programme.title: Queen of Earth
programme.desc: Tensions rise when an emotionally fragile woman (Elisabeth Moss) spends one week with her best friend (Katherine Waterston).

programme.title: Surveillance
programme.desc: FBI agents (Julia Ormond, Bill Pullman) interrogate witnesses who survive the bloody rampage of two serial killers.

programme.title: The Suspect
programme.desc: Two black American social scientists pose as bank robbers to study racial dynamics in small-town law enforcement.

programme.title: The Stanford Prison Experiment
programme.desc: In 1971, Stanford's Professor Philip Zimbardo (Billy Crudup) conducts a controversial psychology experiment in which college students pretend to be either prisoners or guards, but the proceedings soon get out of hand.

programme.title: Freeway Killer
programme.desc: Californian serial killer William Bonin (Scott Anthony Leet) searches for victims on the state's highways.

programme.title: Apartment 4E
programme.desc: Trapped in a dark world of her own making, Piper (Nicole Beharie) has not left her apartment in a long time. A knock on her door reveals a stranger who may have answers to the mystery at the center of her world.

programme.title: Exam
programme.desc: Eight candidates cooperate in order to secure a job with a prestigious company.

programme.title: Nitram
programme.desc: Living a life of isolation and frustration, a young man develops an unexpected friendship with a reclusive heiress. When that relationship meets its tragic end, his loneliness and anger culminates into the most nihilistic and heinous of acts.

programme.title: Re: Born
programme.desc: A legendary soldier with a mysterious past decides to unleash his inner beast to stand up for what he cares about.

programme.title: Prisoners of the Ghostland
programme.desc: In Samurai Town, a ruthless bank robber gets sprung from jail by a warlord whose adopted granddaughter has gone missing. Strapped into a leather suit that will self-destruct in five days, the bandit sets off on a journey to find the young woman.

programme.title: Narcopolis
programme.desc: In the near future, Frank, a police officer, discovers that the legalization of all recreational drugs comes with a price.

programme.title: Into the Ashes
programme.desc: Nick is an ex-con who's adjusting to a new job, a new wife and some semblance of normalcy in rural Alabama. When vengeful former associates track him down, he's soon forced back into the desperate and violent life that he thought he left behind.

programme.title: Centigrade
programme.desc: A man and his pregnant wife become trapped in their car when a storm buries them underneath layers of snow and ice. As supplies dwindle and temperatures plunge, the couple must battle the elements and hypothermia in a desperate fight for survival.

programme.title: Makkhi
programme.desc: Sudeep, obsessed with Bindu, kills her lover Jani, who is reborn as a fly. Jani and Bindu team up to exact revenge on Sudeep in creative ways.

programme.title: Krrish 3
programme.desc: Krrish and his scientist father have to save the world and their own family from an evil man named Kaal and his team of human-animal mutants led by the ruthleSS Kaya. Will they succeed? How?

programme.title: Bhajarangi 2
programme.desc: An evil drug baron named Araka kidnaps innocent villagers and forces them to produce drugs for him. Fortunately, a forest officer learns of this and decides to put a stop to it.

programme.title: Skanda
programme.desc: A young man renowned for his martial arts skills sees his life take a dramatic turn when a secret government agency recruits him for a dangerous mission to thwart the plans of a group of terrorists.

programme.title: Ragini MMS 2
programme.desc: Ragini's viral video sparks buzz. A sleazy filmmaker sets his next horror movie in the same haunted house-only to stir the evil that still lurks in its shadows.

programme.title: Kisko Tha Pata
programme.desc: In a tale of love and destiny, Shreya faces heartbreak, remarrying despite lingering feelings for her ex-fiance. Can she choose between past and present?

programme.title: Mercury
programme.desc: Five friends hide the death of one who died from mercury poisoning. But when the dead friend returns from the grave, secrets unfold, and revenge might be waiting.

programme.title: A Flying Jatt
programme.desc: Aman a meek youngster who is scared of heights, discovers his superpowers when he is forced to face the evil Raka, who is sent by an industrialist to destroy humanity.

programme.title: Sanam Teri Kasam
programme.desc: When an awkward librarian gets disowned by her family, she unexpectedly finds support from her mysterious, brooding neighbor, sparking an unlikely friendship-and maybe more.

programme.title: Aranmanai 4
programme.desc: Daniel's quest to uncover his sister's death spirals into a nightmare, revealing sinister truths and igniting a chain of terror that changes him forever.

programme.title: Saaho
programme.desc: An undercover agent and his partner go after a thief who has stolen 2,000 crore rupees. Soon, they realise that the case is linked to the death of a crime lord and an emerging gang war.

programme.title: Raksha Bandhan
programme.desc: Lala Kedarnath vows to marry off his four sisters before anything else, juggling family obligations and his chat shop, while putting his childhood crush, Sapna, on hold.

programme.title: Antim: The Final Truth
programme.desc: A poor farmer's son moves to the city and becomes a fearless gangster, clashing with an upright policeman determined to take him down, leading to an intense battle of wills.

programme.title: Kanjoos Makhichoos
programme.desc: Man saves money for father's Char Dham Yatra dream, tragedy strikes in floods.

programme.title: Yamla Pagla Deewana 2
programme.desc: The trio is back with double the fun, double the action and double the romance!

programme.title: Mera Badla - Revenge
programme.desc: Family haunted after winning portrait prize seeks Acharya and Dr. Vijay's aid.

programme.title: Makkhi
programme.desc: Sudeep, obsessed with Bindu, kills her lover Jani, who is reborn as a fly. Jani and Bindu team up to exact revenge on Sudeep in creative ways.

programme.title: Indian
programme.desc: DCP Raj Shekhar Azad nabs terrorist Wasim Khan, but uncovers deep police corruption involving his father-in-law. Framed for murder, he must clear his name and fight for justice.

programme.title: Krack
programme.desc: Three notorious criminals clash with Potharaju Shankar, a hot-tempered cop, in a series of intense confrontations that ultimately lead them behind bars.

programme.title: Krantiveer
programme.desc: Pratap is guided by journalist Megha Dixit to empower his village, aiming for independence and self-reliance. They work together to spark local change and resilience.

programme.title: Big Dhamaka
programme.desc: Trouble ensues when two lookalikes from varying backgrounds fall for the same girl. However, an unsuspecting connection between the duplicates further complicates their situation.

programme.title: Zeher
programme.desc: Siddharth, a soon-to-be-divorced police officer, embarks on an affair with Anna. When she's found dead, he confronts unforeseen consequences, unraveling a web of deceit and danger.

programme.title: Article 15
programme.desc: In the rural heartlands of India, an upright police officer sets out on a crusade against violent caste-based crimes and discrimination.

programme.title: Lingaa
programme.desc: Lingaa, once a petty thief, reforms after learning his grandfather built a village dam. He dedicates himself to protecting the dam from a corrupt minister's destructive plans.

programme.title: Suriya S3
programme.desc: Singham returns to confront a powerful toxic-waste tycoon, risking everything to protect citizens. Can he overcome hurdles and deliver justice for the city's health scares?

programme.title: Joggers' Park
programme.desc: Chaos erupts when former judge Jyotin Chatterjee falls for Jenny, a young woman.

programme.title: Chakravyuh
programme.desc: Kabir infiltrates a Maoist organisation to help Adil Khan, who is a police officer and his friend.

programme.title: Sniff
programme.desc: A laboratory accident enhances Sunny's olfactory abilities, and he turns into a superhero.

programme.title: Footfairy
programme.desc: A CBI officer investigates murders committed by a serial killer with a foot fetish.

programme.title: Udta Punjab
programme.desc: The lives of four individuals intertwine during an era of drug abuse in Punjab, India.

programme.title: Firaaq
programme.desc: The victims of the 2002 Gujarat riots grapple with the aftermath of the devastation.

programme.title: The Kashmir Files
programme.desc: A young college student sets out to uncover the truth behind his parents' death.

programme.title: Judgementall Hai Kya
programme.desc: Bobby rents out a portion of her house to a young couple but is suspicious of the man.

programme.title: Omerta
programme.desc: Ahmed Omar Saeed Sheikh kidnaps and murders Wall Street Journal journalist Daniel Pearl.

programme.title: Biwi No.1
programme.desc: Prem's double life as devoted husband/father clashes with Roopali's temptations; Pooja's ultimatum forces a choice between stability and restless longing. Will he choose?

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. Three members of the Brighton and Hove (Actually) Gay Men's Chorus square up to a trio of postgraduates of Brasenose College, Oxford. They compete to draw together the connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random.

programme.title: Stonehenge: The Lost Circle Revealed
programme.desc: In a world exclusive, Professor Alice Roberts follows a decade-long historical quest using cutting-edge research that reveals Stonehenge’s oldest secret.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: Two more teams begin their quarter-final campaigns, hoping to secure the first of the two wins they need to progress to the semi-finals. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. Three philosophy students pit their wisdom against a trio of Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy fans. They compete to draw together the connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random.

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox questions whether we are alone in the universe. There might be more planets than stars in our galaxy - but will we find a second Earth?

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: A look at the extraordinary popularity of both Hitler and the Nazis, as well as the astonishing degree of voluntary support ordinary Germans gave the infamous Gestapo. Despite the popular mythology of Nazi efficiency, Dr Gunter Lohse, a former German Foreign Office official and member of the Nazi party, recalls that behind the scenes 'it was total chaos'.

programme.title: One Foot in Arthur's Seat
programme.desc: Actor Ian Richardson walks up the hill to Arthur's Seat, which overlooks the city of Edinburgh, as he did during lunch hours as a bored office clerk 50 years ago.

programme.title: Remembers...
programme.desc: As part of Pride Month, W Stephen Gilbert looks back on the BBC drama Only Connect that he commissioned and produced in 1979. Today, it is considered a landmark but overlooked piece of television history thanks to its sympathetic portrayal of a homosexual relationship. In this special interview, Gilbert recalls his struggle to bring Only Connect to the screen, the challenges of making an openly gay drama for mainstream television and the impact the piece has had in the decades since its original broadcast.

programme.title: The Other Side
programme.desc: Young academic Graham visits the elderly John Bury to learn more about pioneering socialist and gay rights campaigner Edward Carpenter for his thesis on the early UK Labour movement. Graham wants the facts but discovers, to his advantage, that people are not text books.

programme.title: Remembers...
programme.desc: National treasure Alison Steadman tells the story of one of the most groundbreaking and yet also most overlooked moments in British television. Despite the popular perception that Channel 4’s Brookside broadcast TV’s first lesbian kiss in 1994, in reality it was Alison and Myra Frances who first broke the taboo 20 years earlier, in the 1974 drama Girl. Once thought lost forever, due to the fact that not every programme was archived back in those times, the drama later resurfaced, thanks to a random video recording – giving Alison the opportunity to share her memories of how she approached the moment and the reaction it received.

programme.title: Second City Firsts
programme.desc: 1970s drama. Just as she is about to leave the army, Jackie has an encounter with an old lover. Stars Alison Steadman in an early TV role.

programme.title: Vienna: Empire, Dynasty and Dream
programme.desc: Simon Sebag Montefiore tells the story of Vienna, triumphant after the Ottoman threat receded at the end of the 17th century. No longer an outpost defending the west from Islamic invaders, the imperial capital was to become the most glittering in the world. The Habsburg emperors transformed the city from a fortress into a great cultural capital. Vienna became a city that would define the arts; a magnet for musicians, including Mozart, Haydn and Beethoven.

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning from History
programme.desc: A look at the extraordinary popularity of both Hitler and the Nazis, as well as the astonishing degree of voluntary support ordinary Germans gave the infamous Gestapo. Despite the popular mythology of Nazi efficiency, Dr Gunter Lohse, a former German Foreign Office official and member of the Nazi party, recalls that behind the scenes 'it was total chaos'.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. Three philosophy students pit their wisdom against a trio of Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy fans. They compete to draw together the connections between things which, at first glance, seem utterly random.

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox questions whether we are alone in the universe. There might be more planets than stars in our galaxy - but will we find a second Earth?

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This is BBC Four
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The last of the eight quarter-finalists return to play their first of a maximum of three matches in this round. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. Three neuroscientists hailing from Australia, South Africa and Canada scrum down against the intellectual brawn of three rugby fans, as they compete to draw together the connections between many disparate elements that, at first glance, seem utterly bereft of associations. So what connects cream, quotation marks, tennis matches and yellow lines?

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: In December 2015, Tim Peake became Britain’s first astronaut on board the International Space Station. For two years, he had been filming a video diary for Horizon as he prepared to leave. From family life to the rigorous training, this is an intimate portrait and remarkable insight into the world of an astronaut.

programme.title: The Magnificent Seven
programme.desc: After the latest raid by bandits, the villagers in a small settlement in Mexico band together to hire a group of disparate gunfighters to protect them.

programme.title: Parkinson
programme.desc: Michael Parkinson in conversation with stage and screen star Yul Brynner. Featuring film clips from The Ten Commandments, The King and I, The Magnificent Seven and Catlow.

programme.title: American Visions
programme.desc: Robert Hughes considers how the Civil War industrialised society, bringing steam power, the Brooklyn Bridge, plutocrats and an urban underclass. Nostalgia for loss entered the culture and brought the Museum Age.

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: In December 2015, Tim Peake became Britain’s first astronaut on board the International Space Station. For two years, he had been filming a video diary for Horizon as he prepared to leave. From family life to the rigorous training, this is an intimate portrait and remarkable insight into the world of an astronaut.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter, Jamie Theakston and Jayne Middlemiss present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 25 December 1999 and featuring DJ Jurgen Presents Alice Deejay, Cliff Richard, Martine McCutcheon, Robbie Williams and Steps and Westlife.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Janice Long and Simon Mayo present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 18 June 1987, and featuring Samantha Fox, Whitesnake, Tom Jones, ABC, Chris Rea, Janet Jackson, Curiosity Killed the Cat, The Firm and Broken English.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jakki Brambles presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 22 June 1990 and featuring Magnum, Roxette, Big Fun & Sonia, Wilson Phillips, Yazz, Craig McLachlan and Check 1-2, Luciano Pavarotti and Elton John.

programme.title: Elvis Presley: '68 Comeback Special
programme.desc: In this remastered and extended concert recording, Elvis struts his stuff in his own charming and inimitable style, performing a massive collection of his hits, including Hound Dog, All Shook Up, Heartbreak Hotel, Are You Lonesome Tonight?, That’s All Right, Jailhouse Rock, Blue Suede Shoes, Don’t Be Cruel and Love Me Tender.

programme.title: Chuck Berry in Concert
programme.desc: Legendary rock 'n' roller Chuck Berry performs at the BBC Television Theatre in 1972. Johnny B Goode, Roll Over Beethoven and Nadine are just some of the highlights of this concert, shown in an extended cut. This version includes, for the first time, an epic rendition of My Ding-a-Ling that carries all before it and raises innuendo to an art form.

programme.title: Tales of Rock 'n' Roll: Heartbreak Hotel
programme.desc: A look at Elvis Presley's first million-selling release, Heartbreak Hotel. This film tracks down the song's writers, schoolteacher Mae Axton and country musician Tommy Durden, and discovers that Heartbreak Hotel was inspired by a real-life suicide in Miami in 1955.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gail Porter, Jamie Theakston and Jayne Middlemiss present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 25 December 1999 and featuring DJ Jurgen Presents Alice Deejay, Cliff Richard, Martine McCutcheon, Robbie Williams and Steps and Westlife.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Adrienne Murray explores how new 3D imaging is being used to support heart procedures. Harriet Bradshaw finds out how gym equipment is being invented and tested to help astronauts exercise in space. Andrew Rogers chats to the game developers pitching the next big thing at the London Gaming Festival. And Shiona McCallum meets the medical expert testing out the future of prosthetics following his own near-fatal cycling accident.

programme.title: Asia Specific
programme.desc: Australia's government is changing tax rules in a bid to help younger first time home buyers get on to the property ladder. High house prices is a discussion all across Asia-Pacific, where major cities grapple with high demand and low supply, but which measures actually work to make houses more affordable? Host Mariko Oi speaks to Asia Specific podcast editor Bill Birtles and BBC journalist Abhiram Subramaniam.

programme.title: The Climate Question
programme.desc: A global programme that reflects the variety of takes on climate change, how best to understand it and the world's attempts to avert it, temper it or adapt to it. It is not about questioning whether climate change is happening, it's about finding the best ways to respond to it. This is sharp-edged, analytical inquiry. Hard scrutiny, touched with a sense of adventure and discovery, and where we can find it, hope. It includes stories from across the world on why we find it so hard to save our own planet, and how we might change that.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: Not long ago, artificial intelligence was being sold as a science-fiction reality, a force that could transform society, cure disease, and remake the economy. Now, the mood is shifting. The companies driving the boom are racing for investment while grappling with a tougher reality: AI is costly, unpredictable, and not yet profitable. Even some of its creators warn progress may be too fast, as public anxiety grows and political resistance takes shape. Is AI now at a tipping point - caught between financial pressure, technological risk and a rising backlash?

programme.title: Election 26
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg is your guide as three parliamentary constituencies choose a new MP. The BBC's political editor, Chris Mason, is at the count in Makerfield, where the mayor of Greater Manchester, Andy Burnham, is seeking a return to Westminster. Scotland correspondents are live in Aberdeen and Arbroath, as the SNP tries to hold two marginal seats that it won at the 2024 General Election. Political correspondent Alex Forsyth is in the studio, keeping her sharp eye across the latest political reaction. Professor Sir John Curtice analyses the key electoral trends. And a panel of senior politicians give their reactions to the verdicts.

programme.title: Talking Business

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Austria and Jordan play their opening match in Group G at the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium. Jordan are making their first ever appearance at a World Cup. Austria have qualified for the first time since 1998. Reigning champions Argentina and Algeria make up the rest of Group J.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets the mother and son who became Doorbell Detectives and shared evidence with neighbours to bring a brazen parcel thief to justice. A pair of dodgy delivery drivers attempt to cover up a theft live on doorbell camera as their customer watches on helpless. And former police inspector Anton Sullivan reveals that for one neighbourhood, when it comes to deliveries, there’s no such thing as a safe space.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin struggles to keep his balance at a Liverpool house that feels like it’s sloping away. Jacqui is in Kent, where damp is all around. In London, Tommy thinks a layout change is in order at a one-bedroom flat.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley presents today’s show from Hemswell in Lincolnshire, with the reds and the blues led by Izzie Balmer and Mark Stacey. Both teams are keen to make a profit at auction, but who will win? Meanwhile, Caroline hits the road to learn about the small bubble cars that were once very popular on British roads.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: An island-wide operation targeting antisocial behaviour uncovers drugs in a park, a man with outstanding warrants is pursued, and we learn that PC Miss Skerritt is leaving the force.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: In the hub, Rav, Nick and the team watch on as a man in the north of England struggles with a scammer posing as an internet supplier. As his banking and personal information are exposed to the fraudsters, the ground team face a race against time to find him before he loses money. It’s a fraught situation involving knocking on multiple doors to try to track down the right address and the right person before it’s too late.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Finally able to search for their forever home and a more settled way of life, a former royal marine and a retired civil servant have £375,000 to spend and the expert guidance of Jules Hudson. Jules has a tempting selection of countryside properties to unveil for the couple, who’ve chosen to relocate closer to family in the wonderfully evocative county of Northumberland.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Lee Burkhill and Chris Hull are in Northampton to transform Jackie’s bland and inaccessible garden into a place where she can drink tea with her best mate Debbie. The garden needs to be wheelchair-friendly for Jackie, a relaxing place full of colour where she can potter to her heart's content. Charlie Dimmock and Flo Headlam visit Southampton to transform Sarah and Phill’s long-overgrown and unloved plot into a gothic-inspired garden. They would love a space full of dark, haunting plants and features, and a 'catio' for their two Siamese cats.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage of the opening match in Group K as Portugal face the Democratic Republic of Congo in Houston. Portugal come into the tournament ten years on from winning Euro 2016, and they are highly fancied to go deep into this World Cup. Head coach Roberto Martinez has a hugely talented group of players to choose from, including PSG midfielder Vitinha, 41-year-old Cristiano Ronaldo and Manchester United captain Bruno Fernandes. The Democratic Republic of Congo featured at the 1974 edition – then known as Zaire – and return to the World Cup 52 years later following dramatic qualification in extra time of their intercontinental play-off against Jamaica. Their squad contains plenty of Premier League names, including West Ham defender Aaron Wan-Bissaka, Sunderland midfielder Noah Sadiki and Newcastle striker Yoane Wissa.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Penny worries about telling Gina her and Vinny’s plans, Chelsea becomes overwhelmed by mounting worries, and Ian fears one mistake could cost him everything.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: When a seemingly simple kidnapping ends in murder, Neville and the team must work out what went wrong and how someone ended up dead.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The Idea of You
programme.desc: Solene runs an art gallery in Los Angeles. At the last minute, she is asked to chaperone her daughter, Izzy, to the Coachella festival, where she meets Hayes Campbell, a member of a famous boy band. Unexpectedly, a spark ignites between them.

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police mount urgent searches for two teens missing from care: a 13-year-old who has failed to return after school, and a risk-taking 18-year-old who has not been seen for five days.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high-stakes quiz, joined by a celebrity team featuring Scarlett Moffatt, BBC Radio 2's Owain Wyn Evans, BBC Radio 1's Rickie Haywood-Williams and wildlife presenter Michaela Strachan as they take on the Bridge of Lies in support of their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie, and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Blankety Blank
programme.desc: The legendary Blankety Blank is back! Bradley Walsh hosts as a panel of six celebrities fill in the blanks and help win brilliant prizes for some very lucky contestants. This time, he’s joined by Stephen Mangan, Kimberley Walsh, Jonathan Ross, Craig Revel Horwood, Fatiha El-Ghorri and Nella Rose.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Uzbekistan were one of the first teams to secure their World Cup finals place a year ago, and they meet Colombia in their opening game in Group K in Mexico City. It’s a first ever World Cup appearance for Uzbekistan, who are managed by Italian former World Cup winner Fabio Cannavaro. Colombia were Copa America runners-up in 2024 and beat both Argentina and Brazil in qualifying for this tournament. The class and flair of Bayern Munich winger Luis Diaz means they could be a team to watch this summer.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets the doorbell detectives whose pet camera helped snare a cat burglar. Shocking video doorbell footage shows a masked gang on the rampage in the early hours of the morning. The theft of a doorbell camera is the downfall for one criminal as he returns to the scene of a crime only to discover the stolen doorbell has alerted the homeowner. And former police inspector Anton Sullivan is the cop turned robber as he burgles a home to help the rest of us stay one step ahead of the thieves.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A pair of brothers tackle their first project together in Stoke, and they're ready to roll up their sleeves to get stuck in. Martel is in Aberdeen to see a flat in need of a facelift. And in Suffolk, we get reacquainted with an old friend of the show who loves tackling a bungalow.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian heads to Lancashire, where experts Izzie Balmer and Colin Young help the teams find the best items to sell at auction. Danny also visits the British Commercial Vehicle Museum in Leyland to see several vehicles of special significance.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Policing Paradise
programme.desc: A suspicious package leads to the arrest of a man at the cruise ship terminal. Plus, a double bike collision involving two sets of tourists and a medical emergency at a street festival.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: The interceptors take on scammers posing as the police, who even use fake recordings of walkie-talkie chatter to frighten their targets into believing they are genuinely speaking to the authorities. To avoid the suspicion of a very paranoid, aggressive scam group, the hub team send out the ground interceptors to avoid detection.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse is in Suffolk, helping a couple find a new home in a more rural setting, where they can enjoy retired life together, closer to the coast, lakes or waterways. Also on the shopping list is a large, sociable kitchen, at least three bedrooms for hosting guests and a welcoming community close to hand.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill transform a plot that is just grass, two patios, a shed and a tree stump into Annie and Alex's dream garden, full of bees, birdsong and wildlife.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Czech Republic play their second game in Group A against South Africa at Atlanta Stadium. Since losing in the final for a second time in 1962 as Czechoslovakia, they have only once made it past the initial group stage – reaching the quarter-finals in 1990. This is their first World Cup in 20 years. They could rely on the experience of West Ham’s Tomas Soucek in midfield. Despite not getting out of the group stage in any of their three previous World Cups, the Bafana Bafana have only lost three of their nine matches, and famously beat holders France in 2010.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chelsea receives an unexpected offer, Ian turns to Kathy for help, and Bea meddles with Honey and Billy.

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Police launch high-risk searches for two missing women - 22-year-old Santra was last seen on CCTV 10 days ago, while 52-year-old Laura vanished without trace two weeks ago.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host’s chair for the award-winning comedy panel show, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell returning as the lightning-quick team captains. Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting fact from fiction this week are guests Jo Brand, Sam Quek, Bobby Seagull and Joe Thomas.

programme.title: Election 26
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg is your guide as three parliamentary constituencies choose a new MP. The BBC's political editor, Chris Mason, is at the count in Makerfield, where the mayor of Greater Manchester, Andy Burnham, is seeking a return to Westminster. Scotland correspondents are live in Aberdeen and Arbroath, as the SNP tries to hold two marginal seats that it won at the 2024 General Election. Political correspondent Alex Forsyth is in the studio, keeping her sharp eye across the latest political reaction. Professor Sir John Curtice analyses the key electoral trends. And a panel of senior politicians give their reactions to the verdicts.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Doorbell Detectives
programme.desc: Matt Allwright meets the husband and wife doorbell detectives who used their doorbell video to follow the clue from a criminal’s tattoo and get the men who robbed their neighbour put behind bars. More footage shows how two masked men trapped a couple in their home just to steal the catalytic converter from a prized vehicle. And former police inspector Anton Sullivan shows the one simple way you can prevent your car being broken into.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Featuring a Kent house with not one, but two front doors. Will it be twice as much work or double the profit? Plus, two fascinating Welsh auction lots.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: In Devon, Christina Trevanion learns the incredible story of the World War II soldier who bravely played the bagpipes as bullets rained down on British commandos during the D-Day landings in Normandy. Bill Millin’s son John explains how his Scottish father was ordered to play his pipes as troops landed on Sword Beach on 6 June 1944 – and how fearless Bill unwittingly mooned German troops in the process! A battle of a very different kind is taking place in Exeter among the antiques stalls as two teams of friends go bargain-hunting with experts Ben Cooper and Gary Pe. Long-time pals Sue and Jane have animal collectibles on their shopping list, while mates Ben and Kieran are on a mission to make the biggest profit on the show ever. As the hour ticks down, the blue team make Gary laugh when they mistake a Victorian washing dolly for a toilet plunger. Over with the reds, Ben is lost for words when he spots a tantalus, and tension rises at the auction when the winning team is announced.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Under the Vines
programme.desc: At a wine conference, Tippy is in the spotlight, Louis learns of Philippe’s past, and Daisy makes a drunken move.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton relishes being in his home county of West Sussex as he shows off some of its many delights including, of course, a selection of beautiful properties. He is with a married couple who want to follow in his footsteps by escaping London in search of a new life and home in the countryside.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Frances Tophill are in Leicester to help Pritesh and Mita transform their run-down plot, which is cluttered with discarded furniture, uneven patio areas and struggling plants, into a garden inspired by India, with blooming marigolds and homegrown vegetables. Pritesh and Mita dream of a patio and pergola for meals and entertaining their extended family, a water feature and a cosy seating area. They are also hoping for raised beds to revive their vegetable patch and flowers, a cherished project for the space that used to be looked after by their late grandmother. With Mita’s health declining and daily life demanding their attention, the garden has fallen into disrepair. Now, they hope to restore it as a beautiful, vibrant space that honours their grandmother’s memory and brings the family together. Can the Garden Rescue team turn this neglected plot into a flourishing Indian-inspired garden on a £5,500 budget?

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Martin from Morecambe has always been obsessed with the world of entertainment. Well, his uncle was a lion tamer after all! So, after sharing the bill with greats like Ken Dodd and Danny La Rue, he was bitten by the performing bug and settled in the showbiz capital of Blackpool. An avid collector with a penchant for puppets, Martin’s home has gradually been taken over by a whole host of circus-style paraphernalia, and it’s time to evict some. Izzie Balmer and Robin Johnson are on hand to clear some space and raise some funds so that Martin can fulfil his dream of launching a puppet museum. Izzie has just one day to work her way through Martin’s eclectic bonkers collection, while Robin will be adding extra value restoring those items in need of a bit of TLC, from a ventriloquist puppet who has lost his strings to an out-of-key musical machine. There’s no denying today’s challenges are unique.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: The brainy quiz returns - but this time host Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Lucy Porter. As always, contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as host nation USA face Australia at the Seattle Stadium in their second game in Group D. Coached by former Tottenham boss Mauricio Pochettino, the USA are making a twelfth appearance at the tournament, with their best result being a semi-final appearance back in the very first World Cup in 1930. Australia reached the last 16 in Qatar four years ago before being knocked out by the eventual winners Argentina. These two teams have never met before at the World Cup.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Bruce gets an offer that's hard to refuse, Serena finds out how much everyone loves a baby, and Matt helps Ron log on to his computer.

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: The birthing centre is finally ready to open, and Alex is helping Joyce hire a head midwife, but Joyce has a problem with one of the applicants. Bruce joins Matt for his first ambulance ride-along. Serena and Ron butt heads over the emergency ward's best pen.

programme.title: Road House
programme.desc: Ex-UFC fighter Dalton is persuaded to take a job as a bouncer at a Florida Keys road house, only to learn that the bar is being terrorised by a gang working for a local crime boss.

programme.title: Disclosure
programme.desc: Around one in seven people in the UK are on antidepressants, but do we know all we need to about the risks and benefits of taking this medication? Anton Ferrie investigates the impact of these pills on the minds and bodies of the thousands of young people who take them and meets those for whom their use has had profound consequences. Some say these drugs have saved their lives; others that they’ve changed them permanently, wiping out their sex lives and numbing them to the world around them.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Single, penniless poet Ruby has recently moved back home to live with her mum, Jen. When their strict Presbyterian neighbours descend on the home for her spoiled sister Eliza's engagement party, Ruby’s only job is to not shame the family...

programme.title: Walking With Dinosaurs
programme.desc: Sixty-six million years ago, an orphaned baby triceratops roamed the lush world of Laramidia, alone and vulnerable. Stalked by the fearsome Tyrannosaurus rex, the youngster relied on her wits and diminutive size to survive. Today, paleontologists in Montana are uncovering her fossils, allowing us to imagine her life and the dangers she faced. Stunning VFX recreate her encounters, from escaping a giant pterosaur to a heart-stopping face-off with T. rex, bringing her story vividly to life.

programme.title: Walking With Dinosaurs
programme.desc: North Africa, 100 million years ago. A Spinosaurus father must embark on a dangerous voyage to feed his babies. To reach the destination, he must lead his young family across one of the deadliest environments in earth’s history: a land crawling with ferocious predators. At the end of their journey, an extraordinary feast awaits - if he can run the gauntlet without his babies becoming someone else’s meal.

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The ruined temples of Sri Lanka are home to Gremlin, a baby toque macaque. With predators nearby and a rivalry with other monkeys brewing, she must quickly learn how to survive.

programme.title: Walking With Dinosaurs
programme.desc: Utah, 130 million years ago. A band of armoured dinosaurs battle to reach adulthood, pursued by some of the most formidable predators that have ever lived: utahraptors. It’s a deadly game of cat and mouse that pits heavy armour against vicious claws and lethal cunning; a life-and-death struggle that builds to a final showdown amongst the flames of a burning forest.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May test the Aston Martin Rapide, the Porsche Panamera Turbo and the Maserati Quattroporte as they attempt to find the world's greatest four-seat supercar. After an early morning start in the City of London, the boys embark on a road trip across the south of England, stop off for a race around a closed-off business park, and end up undertaking a rather special chauffeuring job. Meanwhile, Richard is on the test track in two very different muscle cars, the Chevrolet Camaro SS and Mercedes E63 AMG. And there's a double dose of celebrity as Harry Potter star Rupert Grint and F1 ace Rubens Barrichello take their turns as the Stars in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Children of Men
programme.desc: 2027. In a chaotic future, children are no longer being born because of a years-long fertility crisis. Borders are closed, and Britain has become a brutal police state, with immigrants herded into cages and treated like animals. Government employee Theo receives a message from someone from his past, asking for help getting a refugee some papers. But Theo is soon drawn into something much bigger than himself. Kee is not just a refugee needing help - she is pregnant.

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: After being invited to a bar crawl on Khaosan Road, Zara gets under the skin of Bangkok’s notorious red light district, Soi Cowboy - but filming is swiftly shut down by local bar owners and the police. When Zara meets Mike Yu, a young British expat living a life of luxury in the Thai capital, she realises there is more to the city than meets the eye. Meanwhile, a visit to the Bangkok Immigration Detention Centre exposes how easy it is to get caught on the wrong side of the law in Thailand.

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: As she explores more of Thailand, Zara visits Pattaya, where she meets Mac, a British YouTuber lifting the lid on Asia’s sex capital. However, Annie, a local sex worker, reveals the harsh reality of working in the illegal sex industry. After journeying to the island of Ko Phangan, Zara finds her paradise: beautiful beaches and tranquil temples, but Lucio, a Brit who built a burgeoning cannabis business here whilst the drug was decriminalised, reveals that navigating the local law isn’t always easy. Hugo shares his shocking near-death experience that exposes how the island plays by its own rules.

programme.title: Thailand: The Dark Side of Paradise
programme.desc: Zara experiences Ko Phangan’s world-famous Full Moon Party, but beneath the cheap cocktails, music and fire shows, there is danger lurking as the medical team treat a possible drink-spiking victim. Finally, Zara journeys to Phuket, where she meets Dave, an expat who shows off the island’s high-end beach clubs. She also meets Shakira, a tourist in trouble with the Thai police who reveals how easily dream holidays can go wrong. Zara then discovers the tragic case of a British holidaymaker who recently lost their life on the island and whose family is still searching for answers. A final catch-up with backpacker Loukas exposes the true price of letting loose in Thailand.

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Liz has disappeared, leaving a vacuum at the gym that Andy and Jay compete to fill. With his body positivity classes struggling and his relationship drifting, can Andy afford to lose?

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Andy starts at the new gym, but he is soon spooked by high-energy fitness influencer Kara’s talk of high standards. Will he be fired in his first week? And will his long-distance bromance with Spooner survive?

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: It’s mental health week at the gym, and Andy is worried that his relationship with Georgia is drifting. Will her suggestion of a threesome fix things? Or will Andy ruin everything?

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Andy is dumped and embarks on a brutal ‘revenge body’ diet. Spooner and Blue hold a wild house party to reclaim their cool. Will Andy’s nerve hold? Or will we see the return of the legendary 'Mad' Andy and his firework?

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Andy is (sort of) diagnosed with low testosterone and is in a funk. Kara endorses a pre-workout that has unfortunate side effects. Jonathan and Eunice start a steamy affair. Can Andy get his manly mojo back?

programme.title: Peacock
programme.desc: Kara offers Andy the chance to be a fitness influencer. Free stuff! He and Spooner make up, but Kara has other ideas for Andy, including a swaggy makeover. Jonathan is banned from the gym, as Eunice can’t resist his charms.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A desolate train track seems the perfect spot to end it all – until someone else turns up with the same idea. Awkward.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall heads out to find one of the UK’s greatest predators: the dragonfly. He witnesses the aerial prowess of the hobby and dives underwater with the puffin.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes in search of bat-hunting boas, meets a team working to save the deadly but cute slow loris, and he comes face to face with the king of snakes: the king cobra.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Motoring magazine show with Jeremy Clarkson, James May and Richard Hammond. This week's show is an American special as Jeremy, Richard and James try to prove that a fly-drive holiday to Florida would be more fun if you bought a car instead of renting one. Having picked up some wheels for no more than 1000 dollars each, the boys embark on an epic road trip to find out who got the best deal, encountering heat, flies and very angry locals along the way.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: An all-star cast arrives in Scotland under the cover of darkness for the ultimate game of deception and the chance to win up to $250,000. The cards of fate are dealt, secrets lurk in the shadows, and the game starts faster than ever before.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: Secrets surround the castle. The first Faithful is murdered, the mission causes friction, and some overwhelming evidence is brought to the Round Table. The pressure is on as the Traitors must commit their next murder right under the Faithfuls' noses.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: The Faithful are given an early chance to avenge the Traitors' latest murder. The stakes are higher than ever in the mission, as the players must put their life in the hands of the person they trust the most. A brutal end awaits one Faithful.

programme.title: The Traitors US
programme.desc: A fiery mission results in ten players up for murder. One player has some damning evidence, while another lets their suspicions be known by the wrong person. There is a big swing at the Round Table, and the Traitors feel the weight of their decisions.

programme.title: A Simple Favour
programme.desc: Stephanie, a small-town vlogger and single mother, tries to uncover the truth behind her best friend Emily's sudden disappearance. She is joined by Emily's husband Sean on a thrilling investigation filled with twists and betrayals, secrets and revelations, love and loyalty, murder and revenge.

programme.title: Things You Should Have Done
programme.desc: Stay-at-home-daughter Chi is suddenly forced to fend for herself when her parents die in a car crash. Chi learns she will only inherit the house on condition she completes a list of 'Things You Should Have Done' left in the parents' will, the first of which is, ironically, learn to drive. Meanwhile, Chi's highly strung auntie Karen wants nothing to do with her dependent pest of a niece, but very much wants the house she feels is rightfully hers. Chi ventures out into Ramsgate in search of a driving license and infiltrates the family of a mild-mannered driving instructor in the process.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: A social media meltdown forces Geoffrey to reassess his life and values.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall goes searching for Patagonia’s perfect predator: the puma. After waiting his whole life to see one, Steve sees a tiny cub and witnesses a female puma hunting an armadillo.

programme.title: Shrek Forever After
programme.desc: Rumpelstiltskin fools Shrek, who is suffering from a midlife crisis, into being erased from existence and placed in a sinister alternative reality ruled by Rumpel. Can Shrek restore his old life? The solution is just a kiss away... Animated comedy fantasy adventure.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: The gang belatedly get involved in the Great Famine, the one thing they didn’t need to get involved in. Callum’s new job at the Ballymac Folk Museum sends him down a spiral of method acting as a starving peasant. And he’s helped along in this grovelling subservience by his new boss, tyrannical land steward Phillip. The sudden news, to Jordan, that a million people died during the potato famine causes him to finally develop empathy, and it turns out to be a real pain.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Strange things are afoot in Ballymacnoose: animals in small clothing, paranormal sightings of leprechauns, and most bizarrely of all, Callum gets his first ever match on a dating app. The one prickle on the pickle is that Gwen, Callum’s dream girl, is also in an open relationship with the 50-year-old Grainne, a woman who disembowels chickens for a living. Meanwhile, Gemma becomes obsessed with dressing farm animals in tiny clothing and causes Sammy to spin out in paranoia thinking that his farm has become infested with leprechauns. So Lorcan has no other choice but to introduce Gemma to the dark secrets of the Ballymacnoose Paranormal Society, also run by his da and Frank.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: Life in Ballymacnoose becomes very complicated when Callum accidentally walks in on Gemma and Lorcan trying out a new form of lovemaking. The sight triggers a long-buried childhood trauma in Callum and raises uncomfortable questions about the town’s deeply repressed relationship with sex. Can Ballymacnoose open its heart to true love? Meanwhile, Jordan has officially begun dating his much older lover, 50-year-old Grainne. But as their relationship deepens, Jordan is confronted with another harsh reality of adulthood: he’s going bald. Faced with the slow retreat of his hairline and the love of a good woman, Jordan must decide whether to cling to denial or embrace manhood in all its forms - love, responsibility and mushroom carbonaras.

programme.title: Funboys
programme.desc: As Callum begins slowly unravelling under the strain of Jordan's relentless night terrors about baldness, he stumbles upon a flyer for Ballymacbot Wars - a local robot combat tournament offering a generous cash prize and potential ticket out of Jordan’s bedroom. Meanwhile, Jordan is uncharacteristically uninterested in the whole affair. He's become a bald hermit, terrified to leave the house and expose his creamy scalp to the outside world. That is, until, like a dagger in the night, a hooded burst of inspiration sends him back out into the world… and the robot wars.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Friendship, loyalty, laughter and lots of mischief bring best pals Conor MacSweeney and Jock O’Keeffe into ongoing conflict with their school headmaster, Principal Barry Walsh. Getting caught up in the chaos is Conor's long suffering mother Mairead, something that happens very often as she tries to pick up the pieces after the lads' misbehaviour causes problems. A not-so-clever plan to earn some extra cash backfires, bringing the lads head to head with Sergeant Healy, who has been hot on their heels for some time and is determined to catch them up to no good.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Comedy following the coming-of-age adventures of two best friends. Conor and Jock's friendships with the principal's daughters intensify, leading to a comic showdown.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: The lads persuade the girls to spend a full day with them on a beautiful adventure.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and his mam Mairead’s much loved fridge, bought by Conor’s dad, has finally given up on them. Remembering when the fridge was originally bought in town miles away, Mairead decides that a family bonding session on a road trip to that same town to buy a replacement fridge will be just the thing to bond with her son.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock have been instructed to take up unpaid part-time work experience at Mairead's side on the fish stall in the English Market, bringing them up close and personal with life in the real world. With Jock's extra mouth to feed in the McSweeney household, money is getting more and more tight, leaving the trio to come up with inventive and unsatisfying ways of making food last longer. Determined to help out Mairead financially, the lads start to think about ways they can come up with some extra cash. The freak local catch of a very expensive fish gives the two lads an idea, leading them on a dubious adventure involving breaking and entering, a hilarious struggle and a memorable trip through Cork with their ill-gotten gains. Meanwhile, Mairead and Sergeant Healy are in two minds about taking their friendship to the next level, while Mairead tries to give Healy some driving lessons.

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Michaela Strachan present the latest seasonal wildlife stories live from National Trust Crom, the stunning lakeside reserve cared for by the National Trust. Featuring part one of a special trilogy of short films examining the extraordinary relationship between the reed warbler and the common cuckoo, one of nature’s most fascinating examples of brood parasitism. Meanwhile, Iolo Williams broadcasts live from the Natural History Museum in London, where he turns the spotlight on the capital’s rich and often-overlooked invertebrate life.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon has one chance to tackle a life-threatening tumour in his patient’s liver and diaphragm. A neurosurgeon must save her patient from paralysis with unerring precision.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The two finalists head to Longleat Estate to transform rental cottages, taking inspiration from either the nearby safari park or from the surrounding countryside. They aim to show judges Michelle Ogundehin and Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen how much their design ability has evolved during the competition before one of them wins Interior Design Masters and a partnership with retailer Next for their own homewares range.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock heads back to Devizes to revisit one of the gardens she created with Chris Hull. John and Vanessa had wanted an Oman-inspired outdoor space perfect for entertaining, and four years on, Charlie is delighted to find a garden bursting with life and vibrant colour, with the firepit and social area still at the heart of it all. Charlie also reconnects with Lisa in Bedhampton, whose dream of a Greek-style garden inspired by holidays to Kos and the Greek Islands blossomed into something even better. Finally, Charlie catches up with Luke, who wanted a Japanese garden that would also be a fun, safe space for his young son Jude and discovers how the design has matured into a tranquil, family-friendly haven.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael begins a journey in and around Kyushu, the southernmost of Japan’s four main islands, and southern Honshu. He begins his exploration in Kagoshima, an extraordinary city which sits in the shadow of Japan’s most active volcano, Sakurajima. In the Sakurajima International Volcano Centre, Michael discovers how scientists use the latest technology to predict and warn of future volcano eruptions. He rides one of Japan’s quirky themed express trains to Ibusuki, a coastal town where he goes sand bathing. Inland is Chiran, the site of a former World War II airbase and now the Chiran Peace Museum. Michael learns of 439 young pilots who flew from Chiran to their deaths in the notorious kamikaze missions of the Pacific War.

programme.title: Great Central Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo embarks on a thrilling railway adventure following the Silk Road through the most populous country of Central Asia, Uzbekistan. At the meeting point of east and west, this region has been home to some of the world’s greatest empires: ruled by the Mongols, the ferocious conqueror Tamerlane, imperial Russia and the Soviet Union. Michael begins in the capital, Tashkent, where he takes in its vast, vibrant market and samples popular Uzbek delicacies. He travels on the lavishly decorated Soviet-era metro, which was hidden from the outside world until 2018, when a ban on photographing it was lifted. Michael hears how an earthquake resulted in Tashkent becoming the capital of Soviet modernist architecture, and he makes his TikTok debut with a globally famous Uzbek social media star.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Queen's Tennis
programme.desc: Live coverage from day 10 of the 2026 Queen's tennis tournament.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Ben Bailey Smith, Robin Ince, Lara Ricote and Helen Skelton take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s saga through Norway and Denmark resumes in Larvik on Norway’s southern coast, where he investigates the city’s prized natural resource, a distinctive blue rock named larvikite. From Larvik’s port, he boards a ferry for Denmark, landing in Hirtshals. At the tip of the Jutland Peninsula, Michael visits the 19th-century artists’ colony of Skagen to admire their light-filled landscapes. He then heads south to the pastures of Holstebro, home to some of Denmark’s half a million cows. Denmark produces 85 million kilos of butter and five billion kilos of milk per year. Michael visits the Lurpak factory to see one of the nation’s premier industries.

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Day 1 highlights from the second Test match between England and New Zealand.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Ceramics expert Kirsten revives a very unusual piece of family history relating to a prisoner of war, and a father’s unfinished project holding precious childhood memories gets completed.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig, Alan Davies, Bridget Christie, Mark Watson and Johnny Vegas look at saints and sinners.

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: The hilarious Loyiso Gola introduces comedians Scott Bennett and Helen Bauer to the stage.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Adam Fleming.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: Unspun World provides an unvarnished version of the week's major global news stories - reliable, honest and essential viewing. The BBC's world affairs editor, John Simpson, brings his experience and expertise to bear on the most important international stories. In discussion with the BBC's unparalleled range of experts across the world, he uses their unique local knowledge to go beyond the headlines to get to the unspun truth about the world.

programme.title: In Ten Pictures
programme.desc: Britney Spears was one of the biggest pop stars of her generation. She grew up in the limelight, and like so many music stars, she was defined by imagery. But she was also the subject of extraordinary exploitation and control. Years of personal and professional turmoil would play out in the frenzied glare of the media spotlight: breakups, breakdowns and familial fall outs. From the over-sexualisation of a child performer to the very public struggles with her mental health, this is the story of a manufactured child star fighting for control over her music, her life and her image.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: From coffee-making robots in Japan to bionic limbs in New York, can revolutionary technology transform the lives of millions of disabled people?

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the final day of the week’s whistlestop tour of the UK. EastEnders star Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Apollo from Gladiators, are this week’s players, and this is their final chance to claim glory. As ever, Paul Gorton is the unreliable tour guide, and he’s taking the players to Wrexham.

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Acting on a tip off, the Peelers storm a city centre flat on a drugs bust. Inside, a startled suspect is immediately cuffed and arrested while outside, the PSNI team search the nearby streets for the suspected big-time dealer they think may have fled the scene with more drugs. On Great Victoria Street in Belfast city centre, police attempt to arrest a man who refuses to comply with a search. The situation quickly escalates, and he is detained by a number of officers who use a spit guard and leg restraints. The man is taken into custody, where he continues to kick off. Officers and custody staff surround the man in his cell as they try to get him under control. Later, Stephen interviews the deputy chief constable, Bobby Singleton, and questions him about some PSNI tactics and officers’ use of force.

programme.title: Two Weeks in August
programme.desc: Accusations fly when the housekeeper reveals she saw Jon desecrating her local chapel with ‘one of them’ last night. On a group outing to an ancient temple, Zoe, Solomon and Jacob are caught in a terrible car accident that threatens to ruin not just the holiday but also their lives. As Solomon admits his failings to Zoe, Jacob starts to suspect she is the one to blame for all this. Nat desperately tries to undo a drunken mistake, leaving Jess and Dan alone together for the first time since the kiss.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown heads to Suffolk with a couple from an East Sussex town who are utterly devoted to their three whippets and are desperate to leave their 1960s bungalow. They dream of running a small glamping escape, complete with a shepherd’s hut for guests and a garden for their speedy, four-legged companions. Property picks include a striking mystery house and even homes with more than one staircase! Steve also takes to his bicycle to test out a newly created cycling route that winds through some of Suffolk’s most glorious countryside.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers
programme.desc: Bee and JJ go rummaging in Woking, helping a grieving widow sort through years of zoo memorabilia. Can gold sovereigns and a stone eagle fund a bedroom for Jan’s great-niece?

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: As summer hits its stride at Longmeadow, Monty Don brings a burst of colour to the Jewel Garden with vibrant annuals, plants out tender veg, and shares some practical advice for tackling greenhouse pests. Arit Anderson visits a striking garden in Greater Manchester packed with clever design solutions and wildlife-friendly planting that comes alive by day and night. In Dorset, Joe Swift explores a much-loved family garden reimagined for three generations. In Essex, a gardener has transformed a shaded, north-facing plot into a lush, lawn-free haven, while in Bristol, a Morris dancer channels movement and rhythm into her planting as she prepares for Gardeners’ World Live in Birmingham.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Queen's Tennis
programme.desc: Live coverage from day 11 of the 2026 Queen's tennis tournament.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Ben Bailey Smith, Robin Ince, Lara Ricote and Helen Skelton take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo’s Scandinavian rail adventure reaches Denmark’s second city, Aarhus, an architectural playground with dazzling contemporary buildings set alongside historic half-timbered houses. At the striking mid-century city hall, he learns about its design and one of its architects, Arne Jacobsen. At Jelling, Michael reads the runes to discover the birthplace of Denmark. Once a royal Viking burial site, Jelling has two 10th-century runic stones. Michael connects with King Harald Bluetooth to learn how Norse paganism gave way to Christianity. Michael crosses the Old Little Belt Bridge from Jutland to Middelfart on the island of Funen, once a whaling centre and now home to a large population of the smallest whales, porpoises. At Odense, he is transported back to the time of Danish author Hans Christian Andersen at a living history village, where he struggles to walk in clogs and learns to make bread.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Day 2 highlights from the second Test match between England and New Zealand.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: In Hampshire, archaeologist Ben Robinson investigates how a tiny village, originally meant to be a free port to process sugar, became a bustling naval shipyard. Ben learns that three ships built here would lead Nelson to victory at Trafalgar. Then, in an almost untouched 18th-century labourer’s cottage, Ben gets a feel for just how basic life was for hardworking rural families 250 years ago.

programme.title: Queen James: Passion, Plots and Power in the Court of Britain’s First King
programme.desc: Gareth Russell reveals the story of King James VI and I, his fondness for male favourites and a scandalous murder trial that threatened to blow his monarchy apart.

programme.title: BBC Wales Investigates
programme.desc: You could be a scroll away from finding a sperm donor. But at what cost? With fertility prices soaring and treatment difficult to access for some, same-sex female couples say they have little choice but to turn to social media to make a baby. Social media groups and apps are fuelling a cheap, easy and totally unregulated donor market. But it’s risky: outside of a sperm clinic, there’s no guarantee of health screening, and you’re not legally protected. And it’s not just a gamble for those wanting to get pregnant. According to the UK’s fertility regulator, men who donate sperm outside of clinics may also be breaking the law if they’re storing, processing or sending sperm. But what do they think of this? Meet the men travelling the world to donate sperm, and find out what happens when reporter Gemma Dunstan tries to order a sample online.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Paddy O’Connell.

programme.title: Gods of Tennis
programme.desc: It’s 1975, and two tennis greats – Billie Jean King and Arthur Ashe – are aiming to win Wimbledon titles but also, in their own ways, to change the world for the better. Back in the late 60s, as the women’s liberation movement becomes headline news, Billie Jean King takes up the gauntlet for women in sport. Having dominated the women's game for years, winning several Wimbledon Championships, King is spearheading the drive for equal prize money and equal treatment of women, whilst Ashe focuses his attention on racial equality on and off the court.

programme.title: The Phantom of the Open
programme.desc: The heartwarming true story of shipyard crane operator Maurice Flitcroft, who, having never played a round of golf in his life, entered the 1976 British Open and subsequently shot the worst round of golf in Open history, drawing the ire of the golfing elite but becoming a folk hero in the process.

programme.title: FIFA World Cup 2026
programme.desc: Live coverage as Mexico play their second game in Group A against South Korea in Guadalajara Stadium. This is Mexico’s ninth consecutive appearance and a record third overall as hosts or co-hosts; they reached the quarter-finals on each of the previous two occasions, in 1970 and 1986. Mexico hold the record, at 60, for the most World Cup matches without ever winning the trophy. South Korea are the only Asian team to reach the semi-finals, as co-hosts in 2002. This is their 11th consecutive World Cup and 12th overall, the most of any Asian nation. They are due to be captained by former Tottenham forward Son Heung-Min.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is in the East Riding of Yorkshire with a couple heading home after a life in France, along with their dog Daisy and a serious craving for family, friends and proper fish and chips. With £280,000 to spend, they explore a tempting mix of characterful older homes and smart modern properties. Along the way, Alistair also learns about a local scheme helping to create affordable housing so local people can continue to put down roots in this much-loved corner of the county.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Lee Burkhill transform a plot that is just grass, two patios, a shed and a tree stump into Annie and Alex's dream garden, full of bees, birdsong and wildlife.

programme.title: Hidden Treasures of the National Trust
programme.desc: From the conservation of an apprentice indenture from 1785 that tied a 13-year-old boy to a cotton mill, to the unusual homes of working families at Kinver Edge carved into a sandstone hillside, this episode uncovers the hidden lives of ordinary working people – and follows the teams making sure their stories are never forgotten.

programme.title: BBC News

programme.title: Political Thinking with Nick Robinson
programme.desc: Nick Robinson in conversation with people who influence our political thinking.

programme.title: Queen's Tennis
